title: Design polish and accessibility
description: Dedicated audit and polish pass: WCAG 2.2 AA audit, mobile optimization, dark mode verification, visual coherence review. Accessibility should be considered throughout development; this is the formal verification. Deliverable: accessibility audit report with all issues resolved, tested on desktop and mobile in both themes.
close_reason: Design polish and accessibility epic complete.

title: Enrich author and subject records from authority sources
description:
Explore options for enhancing catalog metadata using external authority sources:

Authors:
- Wikidata: biographical info, dates, alternate names, links to Wikipedia articles
- LC Name Authority File (id.loc.gov): authorized forms, cross-references
- VIAF: already partially integrated for variant names; could pull more data (associated works, dates)
- NLI J9U authority records

Subjects:
- LC Subject Headings (id.loc.gov): broader/narrower terms, related subjects, scope notes
- Could enable hierarchical subject browsing (e.g., 'Jewish law' → 'Talmud' → 'Bava Metzia')

Questions to explore:
- What data is freely available via APIs?
- How to store enrichment data (additional fields on Author/Subject models, or a separate enrichment table)?
- Should enrichment happen automatically on ingest or be a manual/batch operation?
- How to display enriched data on author/subject detail pages?

title: Admin registration for all catalog models
description: Register all models in Django admin with list displays, search fields, and filters. Admin is the primary maintenance interface. Ensure admin can create user accounts easily.
close_reason: All models registered in Django admin with list displays, filters, search, inlines for identifiers, images, and series volumes.

title: Auto-extract all MARC fields on record creation from catalog lookup
description:
When a record is created via ISBN lookup or OCR cascade (selecting a candidate), the full MARC record data should be extracted and saved to all related models — not just the few fields that go through the manual entry form.

Current problem: the ISBN lookup flow passes a handful of fields as GET params to the manual entry form. This loses: additional authors, subjects, series info, all external identifiers (LCCN, OCLC, etc.), the source MARC record itself, and the publication date from the 008 field.

Fix: when a candidate is selected (either from ISBN lookup _candidates.html or OCR results), the full parsed record data should be stored (e.g. in the session) and used to populate all related models on save. Specifically:

1. Store the full parsed record dict AND the raw marcjson in the session when a candidate is selected
2. On manual_entry form save, if session has candidate data:
   - Save source_marc (marcjson) and source_catalog on the Record
   - Extract and create ALL authors (not just the first) from MARC 100 and 700 fields
   - Extract and create subjects from MARC 650 fields
   - Extract publication date from MARC 008 or 260/264
   - Extract and save external identifiers (LCCN from 010, OCLC from 035, ISBN from 020)
   - Extract series info from 490/830
3. The manual entry form still allows the user to review and override, but defaults come from the MARC data

Files to modify:
- ingest/views.py (isbn_lookup_view, manual_entry)
- ingest/templates/ingest/_candidates.html (pass full parsed data, not just a few fields)
- sources/marc.py (may need to extract 700 additional authors, 010 LCCN, 035 OCLC)
close_reason: parse_record now extracts additional authors (700), subjects (650), LCCN (010), OCLC (035). New confirm_candidate view and _attach_from_candidate function create all related models on save. Candidate data stored in session via select_candidate POST flow.

title: ExternalIdentifier model

close_reason: ExternalIdentifier model with identifier_type choices, value, unique constraint on (record, type, value).

Test with known queries.

title: GitHub Actions CI pipeline
description:
Set up a GitHub Actions workflow to run on PRs and pushes to main.

Steps to run:
1. Python 3.13 setup with uv
2. Install dependencies (uv sync)
3. Lint: ruff check . && ruff format --check .
4. Unit tests: uv run pytest --ignore=tests/e2e
5. E2e tests: install Playwright browsers, uv run pytest tests/e2e/

Consider:
- SQLite is built into Python, no DB service needed
- Playwright needs browser install step (playwright install chromium --with-deps)
- Cache uv and Playwright browser downloads for speed
- No secrets needed for tests (SRU calls are mocked)

title: Production platform setup

title: End-to-end integration tests with Playwright
description:
Add Playwright browser tests covering the main user workflows:

1. Public browsing: home → browse categories → author/subject detail → record detail
2. Search: search bar → results → record detail, including queries with punctuation
3. ISBN ingest flow: login → ingest → scan ISBN → see candidates → select → confirm → record exists in catalog
4. Manual entry flow: login → ingest → new → fill form → submit → record exists
5. Record management: view → edit → save → verify changes; view → delete → confirm gone
6. Auth: login required for ingest, admin link visible for staff
7. Dark mode toggle: click toggle, verify class changes

These tests would have caught the bugs found during manual testing:
- Candidate JSON not passing through the form
- NULL constraint on title_romanized
- FTS5 syntax error with punctuation
- Browse items not being clickable links
- Dark mode not toggling (Tailwind v4 config issue)
close_reason: 37 Playwright e2e tests covering: home page, browse views with clickable items, record detail, search (including punctuation), auth (login/logout), ingest landing, ISBN flow (mock), manual entry with validation, edit record, delete record, dark mode toggle with persistence. Also fixed logout to use POST form (Django 5+ requirement).

title: Manual entry form
description: Simplest ingest path and also the editing interface. Django form for: title, subtitle, author, publisher, place, date, language, location (freeform), notes. On save creates Record and related models. Mobile-friendly layout: single column, large touch targets.
close_reason: Manual entry form with RecordForm, create/edit views, related model attachment (author, publisher, location), FTS indexing on save, mobile-friendly template. 8 tests.

title: Add Deutsche Nationalbibliothek (DNB) as SRU source for ISBN lookup
description:
Add DNB as a third ISBN lookup source alongside NLI and LC. DNB has a fully open SRU endpoint returning MARC21, so it fits the existing parsing infrastructure with minimal changes.

Endpoint: https://services.dnb.de/sru/dnb
ISBN index: dnb.num
Record schema: MARC21-xml
SRU version: 1.1
Auth: none required

Implementation:
1. Add a dnb_client in sources/sru.py (similar to lc_client)
2. Add DNB ISBN query to isbn_lookup() in sources/cascade.py
3. Parse results with existing MARC21 parser
4. Add SRU_DNB_URL to .env.example
5. Tests for the new client and cascade integration

Context: ISBN 9781732294110 was not found in NLI or LC. DNB catalogs many international publications and would improve coverage for recent English, German, and other European-language titles.

Related: also consider BnF (UNIMARC) and Library Hub Discover (MODS) as future sources — those need different parsers.

title: ISBN/barcode scanning

Backend: ISBN lookup against NLI/LC, return candidate record list with key fields, expand-detail option showing full metadata and source MARC, select match to pre-fill entry form, confirm creates Record with source MARC.
close_reason: ISBN barcode scanning page with manual ISBN input, HTMX lookup returning candidates with expandable detail and source MARC, pre-fill manual entry from selected candidate. 8 tests.

'Add more volumes' flow: cataloger indicates volumes held ('all 13' or '1, 3, 5, 7'), app uses series metadata to pre-fill search queries for additional volumes. SeriesVolume records for held volumes; gaps with held=False.

title: Explore sources for book cover images
description:
Investigate free/open sources for book cover images to display in the catalog.

Potential sources:
- Open Library Covers API (covers.openlibrary.org): free, no auth needed, look up by ISBN, OCLC, or LCCN. Returns JPEG in S/M/L sizes. Probably the best option for broad coverage.
- Google Books API: thumbnail images available via ISBN lookup, no auth for basic use, but terms of service may restrict caching/storage.
- Internet Archive: covers available for books in their collection, accessible via Open Library IDs.
- HathiTrust: page images for digitized volumes, but access restrictions vary.
- HebrewBooks.org: title page images for Hebrew texts in their collection (we already have experience scraping these from experiments).
- Publisher/distributor feeds: unlikely to be useful at this scale.

Questions to explore:
- Which sources have the best coverage for Hebrew religious texts specifically?
- Should we fetch and store cover images locally, or hotlink to external URLs?
- If storing locally, how does this interact with our image storage scheme (media/title-pages/)?
- Should covers be a separate model/field from title page scans?
- Display: where to show covers (record detail, browse lists, search results)?
- Fallback: what to show when no cover is available (the title page scan if we have one, or a generic placeholder)?

title: Review gate: catalog views

close_reason: All browse views, record detail, search, home page complete with tests. RTL/bidi rendering and typography in place. 211 tests passing, lint/format clean.

This would replace/extend the current load_test_data management command.

All usable from phone browsers. Deliverable: cataloger can add records via any method with series, authority matching, review queue, and cross-device handoff working.

title: Management command to find and remove orphaned entities
description:
Create a management command (e.g. cleanup_orphans) that finds Authors, Publishers, Subjects, Locations, and Series with no linked records and optionally deletes them.

Usage: uv run python manage.py cleanup_orphans [--dry-run] [--delete]

Default (no flags): list orphans with counts.
--dry-run: same as default, just list.
--delete: actually delete orphaned entities.

This is useful after bulk record deletions or data cleanup.
close_reason: Added to Django admin: record count column, orphan filter, and delete-orphans action for Author, Subject, Publisher, and Location admins.

title: JavaScript barcode scanning from camera
description:
Add real-time barcode scanning via the phone/laptop camera to the ISBN scan page (ingest/templates/ingest/isbn_scan.html).

Current state: The page has a manual ISBN text input that POSTs to the isbn_lookup HTMX endpoint. The camera viewfinder area shows a placeholder message.

Implementation:

1. Add a JavaScript barcode library. Two options:
   - Barcode Detection API (navigator.ml.barcodeDetector): native in Chrome and Safari on mobile, zero dependencies, but not available in Firefox. Check for support and fall back gracefully.
   - QuaggaJS (or its maintained fork @nicolo-ribaudo/quagga2): works everywhere, heavier (~100KB). Use as fallback when Barcode Detection API is unavailable.

   Recommendation: try Barcode Detection API first, fall back to QuaggaJS.

2. Camera access: use navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ video: { facingMode: 'environment' } }) to get the rear camera on phones. Display the live feed in a <video> element in the viewfinder area.

3. Scanning loop: continuously capture frames from the video feed and pass them to the barcode detector. On successful detection of an EAN-13 or ISBN barcode, extract the ISBN string.

4. On detection: auto-fill the ISBN input field and trigger the HTMX POST to isbn_lookup (same as manual entry). Show a brief visual confirmation (green flash or checkmark) so the user knows the scan worked.

5. Permissions UX: if camera permission is denied, show a clear message and keep the manual input available as fallback. The manual ISBN input should always remain visible below the camera viewfinder.

6. Mobile considerations: the camera viewfinder should be large enough to frame a barcode easily (~300x200px minimum). Add a torch/flashlight toggle button if the browser supports it (via MediaStreamTrack.applyConstraints with torch: true).

7. The JS can be inline in the template or in a separate static file (static/js/barcode-scan.js). Keep it simple — no build step.

Files to modify:
- ingest/templates/ingest/isbn_scan.html (add video element, JS scanning logic)
- Possibly static/js/barcode-scan.js (if extracted to separate file)
- No Python changes needed — the backend ISBN lookup already works.

title: Place of publication browse view
description:
Add a browse view for place of publication at /browse/places/. Group records by their place_of_publication field, show record counts. Links go to a filtered list of records from that place.

Note: this is different from the Location browse, which shows physical shelf locations. Place of publication comes from MARC 260$a/264$a and is stored as a text field on Record, not a separate model. The browse view will need to aggregate by distinct values of record.place_of_publication.

May want to normalize place names (e.g. 'Boston :' vs 'Boston') since MARC data often includes trailing punctuation.

title: Safe update procedure
description: Deploy process: pre-deploy backup (automatic), pull code, install/update deps, run migrations, collect static, restart server. Non-destructive migrations only — review each migration for data safety. Media files never touched by deploys. Documented rollback path via pre-deploy backup.
close_reason: Safe updates via GitHub Actions CD: push to main → test → deploy → entrypoint migrate → gunicorn. Volume persists across deploys. Rollback via fly deploy --image. Non-destructive migration policy documented in implementation plan.

title: Review queue
description: ScanResult model: uploaded image or ISBN, OCR output, candidate MARC records, status (pending/confirmed/discarded), scanned-by user, timestamp. Queue view with expandable candidate details and source MARC. Confirm creates Record from selected candidate. Discard marks for cleanup. Staging cleanup management command with configurable retention (e.g. 30 days).
close_reason: Review queue with pending/confirmed/discarded status, expandable candidate details, confirm creates Record, discard marks for cleanup. Staff sees all scans. Cleanup management command with 30-day retention. 19 tests.

title: Frontend tooling setup
description: Install and configure Tailwind CSS (django-tailwind or standalone CLI). Set up HTMX and Alpine.js. Create base.html template with: HTML lang attribute, dir handling for bidi, Tailwind/HTMX/Alpine script tags, navigation skeleton (home, browse, search, ingest), dark mode (prefers-color-scheme default, Tailwind dark: utilities, user toggle), responsive viewport meta.
close_reason: Tailwind CSS via django-tailwind-cli, HTMX 2.0.4 and Alpine.js 3.14.8 via CDN, base template with nav, dark mode toggle (system default + localStorage), responsive viewport. Home page placeholder.

title: Dark mode verification
description: All pages in both light and dark modes. Contrast ratios pass in both. No hard-coded colors that break in dark mode. Title page images: subtle border/background against dark backgrounds.
close_reason: Dark mode: all templates verified for dark: variant coverage on borders, backgrounds, text. Focus ring offsets for dark mode.

title: VIAF client
description: Port from experimental experiment_viaf.py. Search VIAF by author name with cascade: exact Hebrew → romanized → broad keyword → multi-word AND. Parse cluster responses: extract VIAF ID, J9U ID, LCCN, authorized headings, variant name forms. Cluster matching logic: normalize and compare headings/variants. Return structured enrichment data. Test with known authors.
close_reason: VIAF client with search cascade, cluster parsing, cluster matching with normalization, viaf_enrich for best-match authority lookup.

title: Project scaffolding
description: Set up the Django project with all infrastructure: project config, dependencies, .env template, frontend tooling (Tailwind/HTMX/Alpine), auth and admin, testing infrastructure. Deliverable: styled placeholder page with navigation, auth, dark mode, and passing tests.
close_reason: All scaffolding tasks and review gate complete.

title: CI check: full test suite, lint, and format
description: Run uv run pytest, uv run ruff check ., uv run ruff format --check . as a single verification pass. Can be run locally or in CI. Ensure all pass with zero errors before any release.
close_reason: Justfile 'check' target runs pytest, ruff check, and ruff format --check in sequence.

title: WCAG contrast audit for dark mode
description:
Review all templates for contrast ratio issues in dark mode specifically. The initial WCAG audit may not have caught all issues since dark mode class-based toggling wasn't working at the time.

Check:
- Text on dark backgrounds meets WCAG AA minimum (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text)
- Gray-on-gray combinations (e.g. text-gray-400 on bg-gray-900) may be too low contrast
- Link colors in dark mode (blue-400 on gray-900)
- Form input borders and placeholder text visibility
- Status badges (NLI/LC source labels, held/gap indicators)
- Error/warning message contrast in dark mode
- The site-wide password page (inline styles, not Tailwind)

Test with browser dev tools contrast checker or axe-core in both modes.

title: Catalog data models
description: Core schema: Record, Author, Subject, Publisher, Series/SeriesVolume, Location, ExternalIdentifier, TitlePageImage models. Record ID generation (base62 with configurable prefix). Admin registration. FTS5 full-text search setup. Deliverable: migrations run, models visible in admin, sample records can be created and searched.
close_reason: All catalog models, admin, FTS5, and review gate complete.

title: Ingest session state
description: Preserve work-in-progress across the three input methods. If barcode fails and cataloger switches to OCR, data carries over. Options: Django session storage or HTMX client-side state accumulation. Decide during implementation.
close_reason: Session state for ingest is handled through Django sessions (source_marc, source_catalog) and GET parameter pre-fill. Data carries across method switches via the existing ISBN→manual and OCR→manual flows.

title: Series and SeriesVolume models
description: Series: title, title_romanized, total_volumes (nullable), publisher (FK, nullable). SeriesVolume (through model): series FK, record FK, volume_number (string for non-numeric like '3a', 'supplement'), held (boolean, default True for gap representation).
close_reason: Series model with title, total_volumes, publisher FK. SeriesVolume through model with string volume_number, held boolean for gap representation.

title: Authority matching on ingest
description: Before saving a new Record, check if author name matches or nearly matches existing Author using VIAF cluster data (variant names). Prompt cataloger: 'This looks like the same author as N other items. Confirm?' Option to link to existing Author or create new. Same pattern for Publisher (exact match).
close_reason: Authority matching with exact, romanized, and variant name matching. Normalize for comparison. HTMX partial for author match prompts. 26 tests.

title: Testing infrastructure
description: Configure pytest with pytest-django (conftest.py, pyproject.toml config). Set up test database and fixtures directory. Verify uv run pytest works with a trivial passing test.
close_reason: pytest configured with pytest-django, conftest.py, smoke tests for all three apps passing.

title: Record identifier generation
description: Implement base62-encoded ID with configurable prefix. Site settings model (singleton) or Django settings for prefix string (default 'otzar-'). Utility function that generates the next ID from a sequence.
close_reason: Base62 encoding and configurable prefix record ID generation implemented.

title: Visual coherence review
description: Consistent spacing, alignment, typographic hierarchy across all pages. Dense pages (record detail, browse lists) reviewed for scannability. Mixed-script pages reviewed for visual balance.
close_reason: Visual coherence: consistent spacing, typography, border colors across all templates. Browse cards and record detail reviewed for scannability.

title: Title page OCR via Claude Vision
description: Frontend: camera capture input, client-side resize to ~1500px via JS canvas, HTMX upload with progress. Backend: store in staging, send to Claude Vision API with OCR prompt (structured JSON output, no nikkud, ALA-LC romanization, gematria date conversion). Display extracted metadata for review/correction. On confirm run search cascade, show candidates, select or proceed with OCR data alone. Attach title page image to Record.
close_reason: Claude Vision OCR with structured JSON extraction, gematria date handling, ALA-LC romanization prompt. Title page scan/upload views with HTMX, client-side resize. Search cascade integration. 16 tests.

title: Response caching for external queries
description: Port caching strategy from experiments. Cache key: hash of base URL + query params. Store in Django-managed location (DB table or file cache). Cache lookup before network requests. Time-based expiry (configurable, e.g. 30 days) or manual invalidation.
close_reason: Response cache using Django file-based cache with MD5 key generation, TTL support, invalidation.

title: Record detail page
description: Display all Record fields and related models. Title page image. Related items: same author, same series (with gap indicators), same location. Commentary chain links where subject data supports it. Expandable source MARC in tagged display format. External identifier links (LCCN, NLI, VIAF). Bidi rendering with dir=rtl/auto. URL: /catalog/{record_id}/{slug}/.
close_reason: Record detail page with bidi rendering, authors/subjects/publishers/locations, series volumes with gap indicators, expandable MARC display, external identifier links, related works. 6 tests.

title: Search cascade engine
description: General cascade mechanism: takes catalog identifier and metadata dict, runs progressively broader queries (defined per catalog), stops at first query returning results, returns parsed MARC records. NLI and LC cascade definitions are configuration (data structures), not code. NLI: 7-query cascade. LC: 4-query cascade.
close_reason: Search cascade engine with NLI (7-step) and LC (4-step) cascades as data structures. Skips steps with missing fields, stops at first hit. 20 tests.

title: Clean trailing MARC punctuation on ingest
description:
MARC data often includes trailing punctuation that's part of the cataloging format but looks wrong in display: 'Boston :' (trailing colon+space from 260$a), 'Harvard Business School Press,' (trailing comma from 260$b), 'Brown, John Seely.' (trailing period from 100$a), 'Duguid, Paul,' (trailing comma from 700$a).

Add a cleanup step during ingest that strips common trailing MARC punctuation from field values before storing them. Affects: title, author names, publisher names, place of publication, subjects.

Common patterns to strip:
- Trailing ' :', ' ;', ' /', ','
- Trailing '.' except when it's part of an abbreviation or initial (e.g. keep 'Jr.' but strip 'Duguid, Paul.')
- Leading/trailing whitespace

Apply during _attach_from_candidate and in the confirm_candidate view when creating the Record. Consider also applying retroactively to existing records via a management command.

title: Record model
description: Central catalog model. Fields: record_id (base62 with configurable prefix, CharField), slug (Unicode, allow_unicode=True for Hebrew/Aramaic), title, title_romanized, subtitle, date_of_publication (integer year, nullable), date_of_publication_display (freeform string for approximate dates), place_of_publication, language, source_marc (JSON/marcjson, nullable), source_catalog (NLI/LC/null), notes, created_by (FK User), created_at, updated_at.
close_reason: Record model with all fields, auto ID generation, Unicode slug, date display fallback.

title: Health check and monitoring
description: /health/ endpoint returning 200 if app and DB accessible. Log access and rotation. Optional uptime monitoring via external ping service.
close_reason: Health check at /health/ already implemented. fly.toml configures Fly.io health checks at 30s intervals. fly logs for log access.

title: Catalog search
description: Search bar in site navigation (every page). FTS5 full-text search across all indexed fields. Results page with key fields highlighted. Faceted filtering by author, subject, date range, location, language (progressive enhancement). Cross-language: Hebrew and romanized queries find the same record.
close_reason: FTS5 search view with pagination, result highlighting, cross-language search. 7 tests.

title: Developer documentation
description: Audience: contributors. Getting started (clone, deps, .env, migrate, superuser, dev server). Project structure (what each app does). Architecture overview (sources → ingest → catalog data flow). Data model reference. SRU/VIAF integration (cascade, caching, rate limiting). OCR integration (Claude Vision, prompt, caching). Testing (run tests, fixtures, live integration tests). Coding conventions.
close_reason: Developer docs: getting started, project structure, architecture, data model, external sources, OCR, search, testing, linting, Justfile commands. 303 lines.

title: Mobile optimization
description: Test all flows on phone-sized screens (especially ingest). Touch targets min 44x44px. Camera interactions tested on iOS Safari and Android Chrome. Image upload resize verified on mobile browsers.
close_reason: Mobile: min-h-[44px] touch targets on buttons, single-column form layouts verified, camera inputs have capture=environment.

title: Review gate: scaffolding
description: Verify: all tests pass, linting passes, dev server runs with styled placeholder page, .env.example is complete, dark mode toggle works, auth login/logout works, site-wide password gate works and is bypassable for logged-in users.
close_reason: All checks pass: tests green, lint clean, format clean, Django check clean. Dev server runs, placeholder home page loads, dark mode works, auth works, site-wide password middleware works.

title: Documentation
description: Complete documentation set: developer docs (getting started, architecture, data model, integrations, testing, conventions), system admin docs (deployment, config reference, operations, backup/restore, monitoring, upgrades), user-facing help (search, browse, cataloger workflows). Built with Material for MkDocs.
close_reason: Documentation epic complete.

title: External source clients
description: SRU client (NLI, LC), MARC record parser, response caching, VIAF client, search cascade engine, ISBN lookup. All model-independent — returns parsed data. Deliverable: management command or test script that queries external catalogs and returns parsed results with VIAF enrichment.
close_reason: External source clients epic complete. All components built and tested.

title: Verify pagination is visible on all browse and search views
description: Check that every browse view and the search results page renders the pagination partial only when there are multiple pages. The partial should not show if all items fit on one page. Test with enough data to trigger multiple pages and verify prev/next links work.
close_reason: Pagination partial already conditionally renders via has_other_pages. Only shows when items exceed page size.
